element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for physical combat throughout the campaign
Core resource management system; players learn feeding drains humanity if done on innocents
Progressive upgrades available (up to 5 levels each); includes mesmerize, invisibility, mist form, summoning, healing, teleport
Players manage up to 3 additional allies with defensive/neutral/offensive stance modes
Supplement to melee; available alongside expanding arsenal
Vampire-specific vulnerabilities; used against player and enemies
Strategic constraint affecting movement and combat timing throughout campaign
Feeding on innocents and negative actions reduce humanity; affects ending variation and frenzy probability
Involuntary state triggered by resource depletion; loss of player control
Opponent can render Christof immobile if staked; critical threat to manage

typical_relief_triggers: Finding blood containers or feeding opportunities
Successful use of invisibility or mist form disciplines
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering armed parties with holy water/stakes
Party member staking or critical health
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based linear progression with resource management tension (blood supply); escalates from medieval era combat to modern weapons in late game; tone shifts from crusader origins to centuries-long redemption arc

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to vampire abilities and blood management in 12th century Prague/Vienna; basic melee combat against medieval enemies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expansion of discipline upgrades; introduction of ranged weapons and holy water vulnerability; party management complexity increases; moral choices become consequential
difficulty_curve.late_game: Modern weapons (guns) in 20th century London/New York; advanced enemy types; culminating redemption/damnation narrative choices affect ending
notable_spikes: First vampiric transformation and blood hunger management
Introduction of sunlight as persistent environmental threat
Encounters with heavily armed parties wielding stakes and holy water
Final narrative climax with three ending variations dependent on humanity score

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign spans 12th to 20th century across Prague, Vienna, London, and New York; location changes mark narrative beats and era transitions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Prague (medieval crusader era) → Modern London/New York (contemporary setting)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game follows strict linear mission structure progressing Christof's centuries-long journey searching for kidnapped nun Anezka and redemption
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Each sequential mission advances central plot; limited player agency in route selection

notable_level_patterns.description: Blood supply acts as primary tension lever; player must balance feeding (humanity loss) against combat effectiveness and discipline usage
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Encounters force choice between innocent feeding (morality penalty) or finding blood containers

notable_level_patterns.description: Christof recruits up to 3 allies; encounters designed around managing multiple units with stance modes (defensive/neutral/offensive)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game onwards; combat scales with party size and ability synergies

notable_level_patterns.description: Vampiric powers unlock and upgrade gradually; each upgraded 5 times; later encounters assume mastery of specific disciplines
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early reliance on basic mesmerize/invisibility; late game uses revive allies and teleport for complex encounters
